name: FLASH POLL (via protectvoting.org)
subject: Gov. Whitmer vs. President Pine-Sol
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Voter Protection Project and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
---
| | _**FLASH POLL ASSIGNED TO Peter:**_ **Who is doing a better job managing the coronavirus pandemic??** | | | [Gov. Whitmer >](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04c3/72412298/4f93dd33/61048c74/2981753672/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=1+-+Gov+Whitmer+gt&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1")
---
| | [President Trump >](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04c3/72412298/4f93dd33/61048c77/2981753672/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=2+-+President+Trump+gt&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1")
---
* * *
**Peter, we think Governor Whitmer has done a FANTASTIC job keeping Michigander 's safe and has been setting an GREAT example for other states.**
**Despite this, Trump 's GOP has been attacking her and encouraging protests.**
Just this week, armed protesters stormed the State Capitol in opposition to her extended stay at home order._Protestors ignored face mask protocol and went off the rails calling Whitmer "a b*tch" and some carried signs suggesting the governor should be lynched._
_**In response, President Trump tweeted:**_
[](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04c3/72412298/4f93dd33/61048c76/2981753672/VEsF/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1")
We think this DEFINITELY isn't the kind of leadership that keeps people safe! While Governor Whitmer has been busy helping save thousands of Michigan lives, Trump has encouraged Americans to inject themselves with bleach!
[](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04c3/72412298/4f93dd33/61048c79/2981753672/VEsC/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1")
## [**But we have to know, what do you think?**](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04c3/72412298/4f93dd33/61048c78/2981753672/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=5+-+But+we+have+to+know+what+do+you+think&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1")
[](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04c3/72412298/4f93dd33/61048c7b/2981753672/VEsA/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://go.protectvoting.org/page/s/VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=6&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1")
_We require at minimum 10,000 poll respondents in order to record statistically significant results. We encourage you to complete our poll within 24 hours in order to reach the most accurate results._
Thanks for your input!
\--VPP

| [Voter Protection Project](https://go.protectvoting.org/page/m/6e4d04c3/72412298/4f93dd33/61048c7a/2981753672/VEsB/?g=ieagVVH8x8Vckyu3dXyiKiA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/protectvotingorg?refcode=protectvotingorg_headerlogo&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=voterprotect&utm_content=7+-+Voter+Protection+Project&utm_campaign=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1&source=VPP_EM_EN_200501_WhitmerFlashPoll_U1_X1")
---

---

name: =?utf-8?Q?Will=20Douglas=20for=20State=20House?=
subject: Will Douglas Named Top Candidate by National GOP Organization
body:
The Republican State Leadership Committee Highlights Douglas in its 'Candidate Spotlight' Series | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/willdouglasfortexas/rslc-press-release-may-1-2020?e=4f010155df)
---
| | [ ](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=dcdddc3971&e=4f010155df)
---
| | FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
May 1, 2020
---
CONTACT: Trevor Naglieri
(508) 826-5135
---
| **Will Douglas Named Top Candidate by National GOP Organization
_The Republican State Leadership Committee Highlights Douglas in its 'Candidate Spotlight' Series_**
**Garland, TX** – The Republican State Leadership Committee (RSLC) today launched its “2020 Candidate Spotlight” series – a monthly program dedicated to spotlighting strong Republican candidates from up-and-down the ballot and from every corner of the country. In conjunction with the series launch, the RSLC also announced that Will Douglas, the Republican nominee for TX-HD-113, was one of the first 20 candidates highlighted.

"We are laser-focused and fully committed to recruiting and amplifying the best Republican leaders from every corner of the country – it’s critical that each of them win this year, and we’re all in to help get the job done," said**Austin Chambers, RSLC President**.

**RLCC Chairman and former Indiana House Speaker Brian Bosma** added, "Winning state legislative races matters more than ever before, and the RSLC is stacking the deck with the most qualified candidates in critical legislative districts. The future of our country and the outcomes of redistricting hang in the balance."

_Will Douglas is a self-made pharmacy owner and graduate of the University of Oklahoma College of Pharmacy. He began his career in Dallas, purchasing small pharmacies and beginning the Crimson Care Pharmacy Group. Will prioritizes the protection of future generations through safe communities and economic strength.[www.WillDouglasForTexas.com](https://willdouglasfortexas.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f04ec3916ea34d520e31df9fc&id=4301335865&e=4f010155df)_
###

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Here's the plan for opening parks and beaches
body:
Your daily Rhode Island COVID-19 update
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
Of all the tough choices we've had to make over the last two months, one of the harder ones was closing state parks and beaches. These beautiful open spaces mean so much to Rhode Islanders, and I know a lot of people were disappointed. But that was what we had to do to keep everyone safe. It's been a goal of mine to make sure that, when we are able to start reopening the economy, our parks and beaches are among the first things we're able to bring back. Today, I want to give you a sense of what our plan looks like for reopening parks and beaches.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001WML6k2BKfTEyiqpsO0qkjiCItg9PYpSHnGkcrjMX2qgWCjV9lvjvtpITQZouKkUrKzRB9e8TKEDTflR5_Bm-8IyHf0Rjtcn3dV-zJdSo3pJaxTzybCVyY8oKI4zUHirT6X_-RMoBCehyq5uKn-XDr7BqfgHE_m0XBkQ8X0gIAxv39DPhtGTy3VNFh5X0c0qaDFVW60Kc55M=&c=i5UTgfVIWMsc8v3sL5jJfzJqN6peyfzQ1X3wl4VsXHzFKO_tlB41EQ==&ch=vDDaYv_n9h4glO6Utf77gyeT-lf-_6XXN_dOLSPv1-Ls_MW-yfVioA==)
---
| To stay up to date with Rhode Island's latest COVID-19 statistics, please check the Department of Health's [new data dashboard](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001WML6k2BKfTEyiqpsO0qkjiCItg9PYpSHnGkcrjMX2qgWCjV9lvjvtpITQZouKkUrKzRB9e8TKEDTflR5_Bm-8IyHf0Rjtcn3dV-zJdSo3pJaxTzybCVyY8oKI4zUHirT6X_-RMoBCehyq5uKn-XDr7BqfgHE_m0XBkQ8X0gIAxv39DPhtGTy3VNFh5X0c0qaDFVW60Kc55M=&c=i5UTgfVIWMsc8v3sL5jJfzJqN6peyfzQ1X3wl4VsXHzFKO_tlB41EQ==&ch=vDDaYv_n9h4glO6Utf77gyeT-lf-_6XXN_dOLSPv1-Ls_MW-yfVioA==) for all the information you need about the spread of coronavirus in Rhode Island.
---
| Rebuilding our economy again won't happen with the flick of switch, and similarly, our parks and beaches won't all open at once. They'll reopen gradually and with new restrictions in place that will keep us safe.
In phase 1, which we're calling "testing the waters," we will begin to reopen some parks, including Lincoln Woods, Haines Park, Snake Den, Beavertail, and Fort Adams, among others. The full list of parks can be found [here](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001WML6k2BKfTEyiqpsO0qkjiCItg9PYpSHnGkcrjMX2qgWCjV9lvjvtm1wSmhoZM5jAmevrE_sHH1xR7Soeb9ZxQAeVEbwr1_RDu5w85AIZuu4GJ8FnMKkpyagFk5VBhg8vXm8BShWD_N1SJpR4t5L65fAwgWJ_dWuPcQUMazbBzc=&c=i5UTgfVIWMsc8v3sL5jJfzJqN6peyfzQ1X3wl4VsXHzFKO_tlB41EQ==&ch=vDDaYv_n9h4glO6Utf77gyeT-lf-_6XXN_dOLSPv1-Ls_MW-yfVioA==). We'll be opening with reduced parking capacity to help promote more social distancing. In this first phase, you'll be able to go to these parks with your family to do things like take a walk, go for a run or a bike ride, or go fishing - the kinds that can be done in small groups. Unfortunately, we won't be able to do group activities right away, like a picnic, cookout, or contact sports.
Now, I know what you're thinking: What about the beaches? In phase 2 of reopening, we'll begin to open our saltwater beaches - again, under new restrictions. Rhode Island has some of the most beautiful beaches in the world, and the prospect of getting to the beach again is helping many of us get through these hard times. Because of crowds, tides and size, beaches are more complicated than parks when we think about how to allow for safe social distancing. Some states that have reopened beaches early have had to close them again, and I really don't want to be in that position. So, stay tuned. I'll be back in the coming weeks with more detail on our plan for beaches.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001WML6k2BKfTEyiqpsO0qkjiCItg9PYpSHnGkcrjMX2qgWCjV9lvjvtm1wSmhoZM5jRJGKsXzrg0B4gg8Fi6KiqmJwOKo2tbE5rFHD2qx8WXPnEebDOJaX3dhPlksBiq2biRZOgvcGtbyY7bejhqyOdeclYaQusyHsrZkGAh--ue3KoLtRVEElELeqL_sH6scEf5I2ncv8YgY=&c=i5UTgfVIWMsc8v3sL5jJfzJqN6peyfzQ1X3wl4VsXHzFKO_tlB41EQ==&ch=vDDaYv_n9h4glO6Utf77gyeT-lf-_6XXN_dOLSPv1-Ls_MW-yfVioA==)
---
| We're monitoring the data, and I do hope we'll be able to lift the stay-at-home order on May 9, but that is in no way a foregone conclusion. I'm not going to lift the order before we know it's safe. As we've done all along, we're going to prepare for the worst and work for the best. The most important thing we can all do now is continue to follow the stay at home order. Hang in there, keep using your contact tracing journals, and we'll get through this.
-Gina Raimondo Governor

clean_body: Your daily Rhode Island COVID-19 update | | | | | | | Hello friend, Of all the tough choices we've had to make over the last two months, one of the harder ones was closing state parks and beaches. These beautiful open spaces mean so much to Rhode Islanders, and I know a lot of people were disappointed. But that was what we had to do to keep everyone safe. It's been a goal of mine to make sure that, when we are able to start reopening the economy, our parks and beaches are among the first things we're able to bring back. Today, I want to give you a sense of what our plan looks like for reopening parks and beaches. | == | To stay up to date with Rhode Island's latest COVID-19 statistics, please check the Department of Health's == for all the information you need about the spread of coronavirus in Rhode Island. | Rebuilding our economy again won't happen with the flick of switch, and similarly, our parks and beaches won't all open at once. They'll reopen gradually and with new restrictions in place that will keep us safe. In phase 1, which we're calling "testing the waters," we will begin to reopen some parks, including Lincoln Woods, Haines Park, Snake Den, Beavertail, and Fort Adams, among others. The full list of parks can be found ==. We'll be opening with reduced parking capacity to help promote more social distancing. In this first phase, you'll be able to go to these parks with your family to do things like take a walk, go for a run or a bike ride, or go fishing - the kinds that can be done in small groups. Unfortunately, we won't be able to do group activities right away, like a picnic, cookout, or contact sports. Now, I know what you're thinking: What about the beaches? In phase 2 of reopening, we'll begin to open our saltwater beaches - again, under new restrictions. Rhode Island has some of the most beautiful beaches in the world, and the prospect of getting to the beach again is helping many of us get through these hard times. Because of crowds, tides and size, beaches are more complicated than parks when we think about how to allow for safe social distancing. Some states that have reopened beaches early have had to close them again, and I really don't want to be in that position. So, stay tuned. I'll be back in the coming weeks with more detail on our plan for beaches. | == | We're monitoring the data, and I do hope we'll be able to lift the stay-at-home order on May 9, but that is in no way a foregone conclusion. I'm not going to lift the order before we know it's safe. As we've done all along, we're going to prepare for the worst and work for the best. The most important thing we can all do now is continue to follow the stay at home order. Hang in there, keep using your contact tracing journals, and we'll get through this. -Gina Raimondo Governor P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19, If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use ==. | | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903 | | Sent by mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov in collaboration with |

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: Opening the door
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
Today, Governor Eric Holcomb announced his plan to get Indiana safely back to business. As he's said all week, it's a measured, data-driven plan...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/thankful-469205?e=1acd9bb3d6)

Opening the Door

Today, Governor Eric Holcomb announced his plan to get Indiana safely back on track -- which I think may be the best state reopening plan in the entire country. It's well-thought out, comprehensive, and data-driven.

Starting this Monday, most of the state will enter Phase Two (Marion, Lake and Cass counties will see a slight delay) social gatherings of 25 people can take place and retail businesses can operate at 50% capacity. In-person religious services can begin again May 8. And restaurants (at 50% capacity) and hair salons and similar businesses can begin by-appointment, in-person services on May 11.

You can read the full plan here. (https://backontrack.in.gov/?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) It's certainly exciting news as Indiana opens the door on the next steps of recovery.

But that's not the only exciting news we've seen this week.

Yesterday, I was pleased to announce that I reached a deal with WISH-TV president and CEO DuJuan McCoy that will bring our state convention in June to the airwaves!

WISH-TV and its news partners across the state will air our convention live, giving all Hoosiers the opportunity to see our Party of Purpose at work. The convention will also be livestreamed, allowing Hoosiers outside of WISH-TV and its partners’ viewing areas to see our party at work and be even more engaged in our state’s future.

In whatever form our convention eventually takes, WISH-TV will have access.

Just like Governor Holcomb is working to open the door to our state, we’re opening the door to our party in new and exciting ways. And I know that together, both our state and our party will emerge stronger than ever before.

#BackOnTrack,
- Chairman Kyle Hupfer
Governor Holcomb's Week That Was
https://www.facebook.com/watch/live/?v=824427538051298&ref=watch_permalink&mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Getting Indiana Back on Track

Today, Governor Holcomb announced his measured, data-driven, comprehensive plan to get Indiana back to work and back on track in a way that's safe and remains vigilant about protecting Hoosiers' health.

This plan has five stages, with stage two beginning on Monday for most Hooisers and continuing through the next three weeks. Stage two includes:
* Hoosiers 65+ & those with high-risk conditions should remain home
* May 4 - Lifting essential travel restrictions & allowing gatherings of 25 people
* May 4 - Retail stores, shopping malls and commercial businesses can open at 50% capacity
* May 11 - Restaurants can open at 50% capacity
* May 11 - Personal services may also open by appointment only
* Office workers should continue working remotely if possible

If the guiding principles of this plan continue to look good -- including numbers of hospitalizations, surge capacity, availability of testing and expanded contact tracing -- then we should complete these five stages July 4. However, that will require the work of all Hoosiers!

You can read the full plan and all the dates here. (https://backontrack.in.gov/?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

But that’s not all that happened this week. On Tuesday, Governor Holcomb also announced a new statewide partnership that will allow for all symptomatic Hoosiers—or anyone who has encountered a symptomatic person—to get tested. They’re setting up 20 testing sites in the next few days, with plans to grow this to 50 test sites very soon. This will expand testing capacity by 30,000 additional tests per week throughout Indiana! You can read more about this exciting news here (https://calendar.in.gov/site/gov/event/gov-holcomb-announces-large-scale-covid-19-testing-for-hoosiers/?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .
To stay up to date on the latest developments, please make sure you're following Governor Holcomb on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/HolcombForIndiana/?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , Twitter (https://twitter.com/holcombforin?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) and Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/holcombforin/?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !

Thank you to every Hoosier who is doing his or her part to stop the spread of the coronavirus and flatten the curve. We're #INThisTogether, and we're getting #BackOnTrack!

Welcome Home, Vice President Mike Pence!

Yesterday, Governor Holcomb joined Vice President Mike Pence in Kokomo to tour the General Motors facility. Together with medical device company Ventec Life Systems, in just 17 days they’ve retooled, trained workers and sourced materials to start producing life-saving ventilators! They’ve already produced 600, with plans to produce 30,000 ventilators by the end of August. And they’re all made in Indiana!

“It’s almost miraculous, but for the fact that it’s Hoosiers that came running.” – Governor Eric Holcomb

“Standing here in the Hoosier State... I couldn’t be more inspired. And I wanted to say thank you, not just as your vice president, but as a fellow Hoosier.” – Vice President Mike Pence

In difficult times, Hoosiers have always stepped up to the challenge -- including this life-saving work! Thank you!
https://action.indiana.gop/get-election-updates-040120/?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID]
Primary Election Resources

Early in-person voting begins in less than a month (May 26)! Are you ready? Make sure you’re ready for Indiana’s June 2 primary by:
* Visiting IndianaVoters.in.gov (http://indianavoters.in.gov/?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) to make sure your voter registration is up to date (the deadline to update it is this Monday).
* You can review your ballot, and check to see the location of your polling place.
* If you’d like to vote absentee, you can now request your ballot online.

For a list of upcoming election deadlines and for instructions on how to request an absentee ballot, visit your new Indiana Republican Resource Page here (https://action.indiana.gop/get-election-updates-040120/?mc_cid=24c7cadd44&mc_eid=f59b25f4e9&mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) .

Senator Todd Young discusses possibility of reopening state in town hall meeting (https://www.wane.com/top-stories/hoosier-legislators-discuss-the-possibility-of-reopening-state-in-town-hall/?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
Senator Mike Braun says businesses must be reopened in a smart way (https://duboiscountyherald.com/b/braun-businesses-must-be-reopened-in-smart-way?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID])
Congresswoman Jackie Walorski helps reduce drug costs as part of CARES Act (https://riponadvance.com/stories/lahood-legislation-reducing-drug-costs-signed-into-law-as-part-of-cares-act/?mc_cid=3e6da02a3a&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

subject: Our biggest goal EVER
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | Peter,

Thanks to your help over the past week, we were able to meet our end-of-month fundraising goal!

But Republicans caught wind of our incredible grassroots fundraising efforts, so they're doubling down and pumping more money into this race. Trump was fundraising again for Garcia last night on Twitter and smearing Christy in the process.

That's why we have a new goal..... *drumroll*

**Raise $1 MILLION online by MAY 12th!** It's our biggest goal _ever_ \-- but it's what we need to finish this race strong and have the resources to save this seat.

But Republicans caught wind of our incredible grassroots fundraising efforts, so they're doubling down and pumping more money into this race. Trump was fundraising again for Garcia last night on Twitter and smearing Christy in the process. That's why we have a new goal..... drumroll Raise $1 MILLION online by MAY 12th! It's our biggest goal ever \-- but it's what we need to finish this race strong and have the resources to save this seat. "" If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | "" | "" ---|--- "" | "" "" | "" We are just a handful of days away from our special election, and so much is on the line. We CAN'T let Mike Garcia win -- but that means we need to ramp up our efforts more than ever before. This goal is ambitious, and truth be told, we might not make it. But we have to try our hardest to get there. "" "" Thanks for stepping up -- we'll keep you posted on our progress. \-- Team Christy "" The number one priority during this time is to make sure everyone helps mitigate the spread of COVID-19 in California and across the country. Our campaign is doing our part by canceling all in-person events, moving to a digital operation, and supporting local efforts to help those in need. Emails like this are a critical part of our ability to run a strong campaign that can win. | | Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress. ---|--- |

name: Bloomberg Philanthropies
subject: A step toward reopening
body:
What Mike and Governor Cuomo announced yesterday

| _You're receiving this email because of your participation with Mike Bloomberg 2020. We'll be sending you updates like this on initiatives and issues we think you'll be interested in. Thank you!_ [](http://i.mikebloomberg.com/u/click?_t=8613ca2a3316453b8f979e9fa5797d01&_m=090ab9d67b724348ac6049e28918aedf&_e=9qmMnwMrjtCnGGc5nmzYyWEjfp5QsmN86OsoaHB9kAgdLNuoxsNj8Vq6qxFQlv0abVBwX_HferPBhBovaCvo4cQV-oHstg7tCiUCCMrTu3WcFTXAKB5JLl1zgcCw6nVkCq1AwS1eyWg9QXo__luhylaT4-5ggEh1YtyyyxW2I0GJFeUWNxyL2KWn0cxVey6nWW21EM2iVXKWxaPM63DAZct_zgra63DshGRcY1B_QqfVMQcZ-faurYIercA5fUzuZ8OhRKEPLibPV7uVIR0ZN39PPzyOJ0PbcA_K_X-ggy4mTBmEZYgUscJOGo8kN-nk) Friend, Yesterday, Mike and New York Governor Andrew Cuomo shared new details on a large-scale contact tracing program to help fight the COVID-19 crisis and reopen the economy. The effort will allow New York State to recruit, train, and deploy an "army" of contact tracers to track infections across the state. The work ahead will be a model for other states -- laying the groundwork for future efforts. Resources will include a world-class online curriculum for contact tracers developed by the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health, and the public release of a playbook for implementing contact tracing in other communities. [**To learn more about contact tracing and why it's such an important tool in this fight, watch this video with Dr. Kelly Henning, head of the Public Health Program at Bloomberg Contact tracing will play a critical role in safely reopening the economy -- enabling swift detection of new cases and prevention of new infections. As Mike said during yesterday's briefing with Governor Cuomo: "When social distancing is relaxed, contact tracing is our best hope for isolating the virus when it appears -- and keeping it isolated."
Bloomberg Philanthropies has committed $10.5 million, along with organizational support and technical assistance, to help build and implement the program. The work ahead will be an important model for efforts across the country.
Watch the video to find out how contact tracing can help us responsibly reopen while protecting public health and limiting infections:

clean_body: What Mike and Governor Cuomo announced yesterday | You're receiving this email because of your participation with Mike Bloomberg 2020. We'll be sending you updates like this on initiatives and issues we think you'll be interested in. Thank you! Friend, Yesterday, Mike and New York Governor Andrew Cuomo shared new details on a large-scale contact tracing program to help fight the COVID-19 crisis and reopen the economy. The effort will allow New York State to recruit, train, and deploy an "army" of contact tracers to track infections across the state. The work ahead will be a model for other states -- laying the groundwork for future efforts. Resources will include a world-class online curriculum for contact tracers developed by the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health, and the public release of a playbook for implementing contact tracing in other communities. | - Contact tracing will play a critical role in safely reopening the economy -- enabling swift detection of new cases and prevention of new infections. As Mike said during yesterday's briefing with Governor Cuomo: "When social distancing is relaxed, contact tracing is our best hope for isolating the virus when it appears -- and keeping it isolated." Bloomberg Philanthropies has committed $10.5 million, along with organizational support and technical assistance, to help build and implement the program. The work ahead will be an important model for efforts across the country. Watch the video to find out how contact tracing can help us responsibly reopen while protecting public health and limiting infections: Thanks, Bloomberg Philanthropies | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- . Bloomberg Philanthropies All content © 2020. All rights reserved.

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Back on Track Indiana
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
Today, Governor Holcomb laid out a phased, systematic, data-driven plan for reopening our state. | | [](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f008cafb05ad41da8673ac73b529dbfe&_e=fX7GyRkBk64IYnE1yTaVDDivX_1wI2NvQz-ViXwt-8me75DvPt6Y82Xqk5fpn1QvuLNlDcK_TLHht-oimVp_ensVzgaXtNf-BcbJWmVnfHszLEi9lJpdJCHnqbEop7xfzYPCW43LhRUziJv0IhH_Rt70ACZEaT2kBxG6erxwSyhoMIu0xhxkiOHMOllLKzte12U57y4yVxLUknvHKpbmTW-aaHsxKBC6x8kHUFhyctPkImesciPkLLNdf_RV2wQ_CMndQn8T3AhcZifIn7yeFh0bbYuPSSIH5egtA_xaWOkWFDIz4if7RiMgDPejRcVf-oOhs8zu8SPTGClTCK98LrdBt7GkdEt67ZxTFHGRCpALB_cnQxr6JV72M2fIw42-) |
---|---|---
Friends,

_**“We’ve developed a 5-stage roadmap that with our collective efforts, can help us reach the goal of having Indiana back on track by July 4th.” –Governor Eric Holcomb**_

Today, Governor Holcomb laid out a **_phased, systematic, data-driven plan_** for reopening our state. His comprehensive approach allows us to get back on track while _**protecting the health and safety**_ of vulnerable Hoosiers and ensuring our _**healthcare system is not overwhelmed.**_

_**“We will follow 4 guiding principles to determine how plans to reopen various sectors of the economy move forward.” –Governor Holcomb**_

Those 4 principles are:
* The number of hospitalized COVID-19 patients statewide has decreased for 14 days.
* The state maintains its surge capacity for critical care beds and ventilators.
* We can test all Hoosiers who are COVID-19 symptomatic.
* We can contact all individuals who test positive for COVID-19 and expand contact tracing.

_**“This is the data that have guided us every day since this emergency began, and we’re not going to let off the gas now.” –Governor Holcomb**_

The governor’s Back on Track plan unfolds in five stages. We’re currently in stage 1 and stage 2 will roll out over the next 3 weeks.

_**Stage 2**_ for getting Indiana Back on Track:
* Hoosiers 65 and over and those with high-risk health conditions should remain home as much as possible.
* Lifting essential travel restrictions and permitting social gatherings of up to 25 people.
* Retail stores, shopping malls and commercial businesses can open at 50% capacity.
* Restaurants can open on May 11 at 50% capacity, bar seating will remain closed.
* Personal services may also open on May 11 by appointment only and must continue to follow social distancing guidelines.
* Office workers are encouraged to continue working remotely where possible

_**Nearly all of Indiana will move to stage 2 this Monday, May 4. Marion and Lake counties will begin stage 2 on May 11, Cass County on May 18.**_

_**Starting May 8th, for all 92 counties, indoor worship services may also convene, following specific social distancing guidelines.**_

If health indicators remain positive, Governor Holcomb will move us to _**stage 3 on May 24th.**_
* Older and at-risk Hoosiers may venture out cautiously. Those who can work remotely should continue to do so.
* Social gatherings of 100 or more may occur.
* Retail stores and malls may move to 75% capacity; movie theatres at 50% capacity.
* Gyms and fitness centers may open with restrictions on social distancing.

If we’re still on track on June 14th, we’ll advance to _**stage 4:**_
* Social gatherings of up to 250 people may occur.
* State government building will be reopened to the public.
* Professional office building employees may resume work at full capacity.
* Retail stores and malls may open at full capacity with social distancing guidelines still in place.
* Dining room service open may at 75% capacity; bar seating at 50%.
* Zoos, museums, bowling alleys, and other tourism and cultural attractions open at 50% capacity.

_**“Our goal is to have all areas of the state reach stage 5 beginning July 4th.” –Governor Holcomb**_

In _**stage 5:**_
* We’ll continue to practice social distancing, but conventions, fairs, sporting events, festivals and similar events may resume.
* Retail stores, malls, restaurants, bars, nightclubs, gyms and personal services may operate at full capacity.
* Restrictions will be lifted at tourism and cultural destinations.

Governor Holcomb will update and change this roadmap as needed along the way. If the 4 guiding principles are not met, portions of the state may need to pause or return to an earlier phase of the plan.

_**More details of Governor Holcomb’s roadmap back can be found at[BackOnTrack.in.gov.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=f008cafb05ad41da8673ac73b529dbfe&_e=fX7GyRkBk64IYnE1yTaVDDivX_1wI2NvQz-ViXwt-8me75DvPt6Y82Xqk5fpn1QvuLNlDcK_TLHht-oimVp_ensVzgaXtNf-BcbJWmVnfHszLEi9lJpdJCHnqbEop7xfzYPCW43LhRUziJv0IhH_Rt70ACZEaT2kBxG6erxwSyjvrpGCqmj0BovWbEAF6YAdec9qMncFUB1Xp2ajmP1EC1Wh2ma0MjdMmVPs2sNoxy0QMAg1XjSjU8sKfRlDj7FLvGlAWCWbD1_BFuzMQiU0Ryh_HIDE7WyvZ_rSRsEio4WLaQ9kLKbDsYLtxi3oxfWKXiYwCEBKMliBCn3MhLwC39xcBNx010hcogpFnkKmrNIMNBYKZGHwwMBUNBc1Srn0)**_

_**"As life starts to slowly return to normal, making progress towards being fully back on track will require constant vigilance from all of us.” –Governor Holcomb.**_

We’re still all in this together. Successfully getting back on track is up to each of us.

_**All of us.**_

We’re making huge strides. We’re almost there.

And we’ve learned from day one of this pandemic that **_there’s nothing we cannot do when we work together._**

#BackonTrack,
-Your Holcomb Crew

subject: Arizona is a special place
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress
body:
We have a community that is tough in the face of challenge, filled with loving neighbors and caring people. And our beautiful landscape doesn’t hurt | | | [](http://links.tomohalleran.com/u/click?_t=63bcf327664141c1a59a283fcc863b63&_m=0aa0c0b147b24744a5e4edcfa21cf8df&_e=yXIEO9tYeCdKom39VctZVF1c3YTSITyrP8Fuahqu0IL4pDWhO2B3W-giIRAxyGDBJTjMKIk304bZ22oX-sts0Pnw632mYZslbsUSQYKMDGAZOGRGZQgizZZdezx4qOK2QdyPcPMJSlUSFKtAVyW58jVqdxZioSHBn9G-FWaLkjDMeBIERwXdWJxgS6h-J3Y10asEQOSRfT9M5-eOoKF23RsJvVDs2Q9AwEYlEZhyei0PMufuyX0jUUbEX97eMSRDcksGcGtIPyqRCgNHDv25dW3Gb03e5ULkFPq2UlO5sb4_g_1WBbLJoqOY35J_C1PRqsvcuVeRHXf3uq8ACV90PSQefiPI5ugtwLff8yHjKb6e_XE5IDmHloziObUDf4Izp0Cip0FkEUqvoI3rDwdfFeslU_17ltpm6kRnoYTTuIjO5sFF67XuE8F_GTgCcM3xP9Au7Ph6nqehcUrSfaPHLnBn2ZGPj3LE6m1etBOolB8%3D) Arizona is a special place. We have a community that is tough in the face of challenge, filled with loving neighbors and caring people. And our beautiful landscape doesn’t hurt either! Since the COVID-19 pandemic began, it has been amazing to see our community come together, even while social distancing, to make sure everyone is safe. From individuals donating to local food centers and veterans organizations, to small businesses who have shifted their energy to producing critical PPE and equipment for our hospitals. **Even in some of our toughest times, we show that Arizona is tougher.** My number one priority is working with my colleagues in Congress to protect our citizens from this deadly virus and reopen our economy to put money back into the hands of Americans. My campaign has directed its focus towards helping those in need by fundraising for non-profits and providing factual, scientific updates on our progress against this new pandemic. We will continue to be a place of support and action for our community. While all of this is going on, my opponents have not slowed down one bit in their efforts to flip our seat and send an extremist to Washington. They would work to cut healthcare, delay climate action and take away a woman’s right to choose. We can’t afford that. That’s why, over the next few weeks and months, we will be reaching out with more opportunities to [volunteer](http://links.tomohalleran.com/u/click?_t=63bcf327664141c1a59a283fcc863b63&_m=0aa0c0b147b24744a5e4edcfa21cf8df&_e=yXIEO9tYeCdKom39VctZVNVd0bVqaoCLScXdTRqVs44wyVOIoCtM_P0xoomsFoKpTBFIhO6iAE-tPJ51NvEy9iBpmCDYR9ePamKNToYIAWVhYWN50YssBNY6P9N_ZPRps6ZjHl8bQX40Q_5w9-C09s5hQsSYn5LUdVCwW8gQOj6U8JwL9rdLrzHCOEWGRgaSC6Nvpn_zBRmmuSkaudwMhE9mTiFNYR2g5fzjZzGVbgrTq_Npt7-DjrtYsguhMLLYZ4XNG76JLKGK8hkhzH_z7I89-4SKZZsCVFTCP5ZzRNZeoXlyALsj5CglWHg68Zd9) and [donate](http://links.tomohalleran.com/u/click?_t=63bcf327664141c1a59a283fcc863b63&_m=0aa0c0b147b24744a5e4edcfa21cf8df&_e=yXIEO9tYeCdKom39VctZVF1c3YTSITyrP8Fuahqu0IL4pDWhO2B3W-giIRAxyGDBJTjMKIk304bZ22oX-sts0Pnw632mYZslbsUSQYKMDGAZOGRGZQgizZZdezx4qOK2QdyPcPMJSlUSFKtAVyW58jVqdxZioSHBn9G-FWaLkjDMeBIERwXdWJxgS6h-J3Y10asEQOSRfT9M5-eOoKF23RsJvVDs2Q9AwEYlEZhyei0PMufuyX0jUUbEX97eMSRDcksGcGtIPyqRCgNHDv25ddtxytW8f2QptMXIbtTnpSy-DWSzjDNoLO4Urv0KGFxGd-JgUCvNU5pzRac7fQp6nyIO1NUlUNL6vifsd2-qrieazdZW6-0Zm5_l3mZium9bm3sFEI6agXGLxiDRnQSNxapoZbvUOHvN31Se_iWVFTR61m6RQq4qG64wKy55QXB12d9bhdcww6factrCCqDuKjmRx2ZfQQybCtOfjNa9BXU%3D) to my campaign. You can trust that I’ll continue to serve as an independent voice of reason fighting to clean up Washington, but we need your support to win one of the most competitive races in the country. If you can’t afford to give your time and money right now, I understand and want to provide you with the opportunity to [update your contact preferences](http://links.tomohalleran.com/u/click?_t=63bcf327664141c1a59a283fcc863b63&_m=0aa0c0b147b24744a5e4edcfa21cf8df&_e=yXIEO9tYeCdKom39VctZVBVeDEcLzl1yVlH4GrUrspDttgvDiBd562JrQmeqNXXwIg0kHYPs2XfqDdoJ_v-pzOoh930S0o0X4xPtullVofYOC91QQEENXPsGLCp53J5_oJn39k03_2UCx0w7tMqr6C-Q0rApObLyhwxsNAo9Aj_yrX8YqI1KGa68WvoPPhNHi-pxbFkfWPfWrTl6lLPCGbi11xfsfev2hLAREq3JqmDUcojm2os-Tu1Dq1-RxqqWWxvTjP20uEqLh_ly76_tVb6Bfum15xBDgOLJJKJmgVY_41cbfoBbvu2erL8rBIrP) until we all get back to work. Most importantly, I hope you and your family stay safe and I will continue to do everything in my power to make sure we all get through these tough times. Ditat Deus,
Tom O’Halleran Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress
